reasoning: Extracting 9 bits (39-47) correctly yields a value in [0, 512). This is a correct and desirable property verified by bit_vector reasoning — it confirms the bitmask is properly constructed.
An already-aligned address returning itself after alignment is correct and expected. `sub(a, 0) == a` is trivially true and this is desirable behavior.
An L2-aligned address (2MB-aligned) has bits 12-20 all zero, so l3_bits being zero is correct. This is a desirable mathematical property of the bit-field hierarchy.
Aligning zero to any positive alignment produces zero — `sub(0, 0 % b) == sub(0, 0) == 0`. This is correct and expected behavior with no underflow risk.
